text.title: validation-methods Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for validation-methods. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Collect evidence from at least 3 independent source types before scoring; single-source validation is fragile.
rule.rationale: Different sources have different biases (Reddit skews toward pain; Twitter toward aspiration; interviews toward social desirability).

rule.statement: Use verbatim quotes as primary evidence — paraphrases lose signal in synthesis.
rule.rationale: Direct customer language reveals emotional intensity and specificity; paraphrases flatten both.

rule.statement: Apply the 5-criteria model AFTER evidence collection, not during, to avoid confirmation bias.
rule.rationale: Bias during evidence collection leads to selective attention and missed contradictions.

rule.statement: For niche scoring, research market size from multiple proxies (search volume + TAM reports + competitor revenue estimates) and take the geometric mean.
rule.rationale: Single proxy is prone to extremes; geometric mean dampens outliers and is more robust than arithmetic mean.

rule.statement: Record your Proceed/Pivot/Kill decision log with the evidence snapshot so future agents can re-evaluate as new data arrives.
rule.rationale: Decision log enables learning and prevents "sunk cost fallacy" as new evidence emerges.

rule.statement: Pain-point mining is most reliable on platforms where users are not trying to sell something (Reddit > LinkedIn).
rule.rationale: Sellers bias their language; genuine users express pain more directly.
